title: Low Light Entry
description: A coat-check groove that lets the room breathe before it moves — filtered sub-bass and brushed hats easing the door open with a single jazz stab as the only landmark.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (brushed shuffled hats, sub-bass filtered low, room tone) -> Build 1 (jazz chord stab enters on the offbeat) -> Drop 1 (groove opens, bassline unfilters, synth hook motif introduced) -> Breakdown (smoky piano fragment over hats only) -> Build 2 (stab returns, percussion layers stack) -> Drop 2 (full warm groove) -> Outro (bassline fades to filtered hum, hats trail off)
soundDesignPrompt: Sub-heavy sine bass run through a slow low-pass sweep so it feels like it's behind glass at first; shuffled hi-hats programmed with swung 16th-note ghost notes for a loose, walking feel; a single muted Rhodes-style stab voicing a minor 9th chord, filtered and short-decayed; no lead synth until the first drop, where a soft plucked hook (sine + light FM) outlines a four-note motif.
mixMasterNotes: Keep bass warm and round rather than aggressive — target a gentle low-end bloom around 80-100Hz with headroom left for the peak tracks later in the set; master loudness should sit a touch lower than the rest of the album so it reads as an entrance, not a peak.
djTransitionNotes: Long 32-bar intro and outro of just hats and bass make this ideal for beatmatched opening sets; pairs cleanly with anything in the 120-124 BPM tech-house pocket; breakdown at the midpoint gives a natural spot to bring in a second deck.
stylePrompt: Tech-house, 122 BPM, sub-driven filtered bassline, brushed shuffled hats, single jazz minor-9th stab, soft plucked synth hook, smoky piano fragment breakdown, warm nocturnal club groove, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: A single thread of neon gold light unspools slowly across a black velvet field, pulsing wider with the sub-bass; a faint plum haze breathes in the background, brightening only when the jazz stab hits, like a match catching in a dark room.
filmPrompt: Slow dolly-in toward an empty velvet booth lit by a single gold rim-light, smoke curling in the air, the camera holding steady as the bass swells until the first stab hits and the light flares warm.
imagePrompt: A single black velvet booth glowing with thin neon-gold rim light against deep plum shadow, a faint circuit-board pattern etched like jewellery into the leather seam, wisps of smoke catching the light, cinematic painterly chiaroscuro, square composition, no people, no text

title: Plum Light District
description: A street-walking groove built on a rubbery bassline and rimshot-driven percussion, evoking the few blocks of neon before you reach the door.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (rimshot pattern, muted bass pulse) -> Build 1 (open hats layer in, jazz stab call-and-response) -> Drop 1 (full bassline, synth hook enters) -> Breakdown (piano fragment + sparse percussion) -> Build 2 (filtered riser, hats double up) -> Drop 2 (peak of the track, hook variation) -> Outro (bass and rimshots only, fading)
soundDesignPrompt: A rubbery resonant bassline with a short envelope and slight saturation for grit without harshness; rimshots and closed hats locked into a strutting shuffle pattern with occasional open-hat accents; a detuned analog stab voicing minor 7th chords answered by a short two-note synth hook that acts as the track's vocal-shaped centerpiece; smoky upright-style piano fragment used sparingly in the breakdown for texture, not melody.
mixMasterNotes: Push the bassline's midrange grit slightly forward so it reads on small club speakers, but keep peak transients controlled for headroom; this track should translate equally well as a lounge walk-in cue and a club opener.
djTransitionNotes: Sits comfortably between 121-124 BPM material; the rimshot-only intro and outro (16 bars each) make it easy to phrase-match; breakdown at roughly 2/3 mark is a good spot for a quick EQ swap into the next track.
stylePrompt: Dark tech-house, 123 BPM, rubbery saturated bassline, strutting rimshot percussion, detuned analog jazz stab, two-note synth hook, smoky piano texture, neon-district nocturnal groove, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: Thin gold circuit-lines trace a slow walking path across a deep plum backdrop, branching and reconnecting on each rimshot, with soft velvet-black vignetting that pulses outward on the bass hits.
filmPrompt: A slow tracking shot along a rain-slicked alley of neon-gold signage reflected in black puddles, camera gliding at walking pace toward a glowing club entrance, plum shadows pooling at the edges of frame.
imagePrompt: A narrow nocturnal alley glowing with warm neon-gold signage reflected in wet black pavement, deep plum shadows framing the edges, a subtle circuit-board motif worked into the metal signage like fine jewellery, cinematic film-noir lighting, square composition, no readable text, no people

title: Velvet Rope Glow
description: The last track before the floor truly fills — a confident, mid-tempo groove with a rising horn-stab motif standing in for the hook a singer would normally carry.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (shuffled hats, rolling bassline) -> Build 1 (muted horn stab motif introduced) -> Drop 1 (full groove, horn stab answered by synth pluck) -> Breakdown (piano fragment solo over filtered bass) -> Build 2 (horn stabs layer and rise) -> Drop 2 (full energy, all elements combined) -> Outro (groove strips to bass and hats)
soundDesignPrompt: A rolling, slightly swung bassline with a syncopated second note for forward momentum; tight closed hats with an open-hat lift on the off-beats; a muted brass-style synth stab (filtered, short attack) playing a rising three-chord motif that functions as the track's main hook; a sparse smoky piano fragment used melodically in the breakdown, never repeating the same phrase twice.
mixMasterNotes: Give the horn stab enough upper-midrange presence to cut through a packed room without becoming harsh; keep the bass warm but slightly tighter than tracks 1-2 to start preparing the low end for the deepening pulse ahead.
djTransitionNotes: Works as a bridge from warm-up into deepening sets; compatible with 122-126 BPM material; the horn-stab breakdown is a strong cue point for layering in a second track's intro.
stylePrompt: Tech-house, 124 BPM, rolling syncopated bassline, muted brass-style stab hook, shuffled hats, smoky piano fragment breakdown, confident elegant club energy, rising motif, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: A velvet rope of glowing gold light rises and curls across the frame in time with the horn stab, deep plum smoke swirling beneath it, black velvet texture rippling like fabric caught in slow wind.
filmPrompt: Camera glides past a velvet rope line toward an open door spilling gold light, rising in a slow crane shot as the horn motif builds, the frame warming from plum to gold at the peak.
imagePrompt: A glowing velvet rope strung between brass-gold posts against a black velvet curtain backdrop, deep plum light pooling at the base, a subtle circuit-board engraving catching the light like jewellery on the posts, painterly cinematic glow, square composition, no people, no text

title: Deepening Pulse
description: The floor's first real push — a darker, more insistent bassline locks in with a hypnotic two-bar synth hook that loops like a held breath.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (looping synth hook alone over kick) -> Build 1 (bassline enters, hats shuffle in) -> Drop 1 (full pulse, jazz stab punctuation) -> Breakdown (hook isolated, filtered down) -> Build 2 (percussion layers return, riser) -> Drop 2 (peak pulse, hook doubled in octaves) -> Outro (hook alone fades into silence)
soundDesignPrompt: A dark, slightly distorted bassline with a steady eighth-note pulse and occasional sub drops; tight, dry shuffled hats with minimal reverb to keep the groove close and hypnotic; a two-bar looping synth hook built from a plucked minor-7th arpeggio, treated with a short delay for width; a single jazz chord stab used only as punctuation at phrase endings, never as a constant layer.
mixMasterNotes: Tighten the low end here compared to the opening tracks — less bloom, more punch, since this begins the deepening phase; keep the hook narrow and centered so it reads as a focal point rather than washing into the stereo field.
djTransitionNotes: BPM-compatible with tracks 3 and 5; the hook-only intro and outro make it easy to loop and extend live; breakdown isolates the hook cleanly for a quick filter-sweep transition.
stylePrompt: Dark tech-house, 124 BPM, hypnotic looping plucked synth hook, insistent eighth-note bassline, dry shuffled hats, sparse jazz stab punctuation, deepening club pulse, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: A pulsing gold circuit-node at the center of the frame beats in time with the bass, sending thin plum-lit traces outward across a black velvet grid that flexes like fabric under tension.
filmPrompt: A static, slowly breathing close-up on pooled gold light against black velvet, the frame pulsing subtly wider with each bass hit, plum shadow creeping in at the corners as the track deepens.
imagePrompt: An abstract close-up of a glowing gold circuit-node embedded in black velvet fabric, deep plum light bleeding from its edges, fine jewellery-like filigree tracing outward like wiring, moody macro photography, square composition, no people, no text

title: Smoke Under Glass
description: A slow-burning groove centered on a muted electric-piano fragment that drifts above a tight, dubby bassline like smoke trapped behind glass.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (dubby bass with delay tail, sparse hats) -> Build 1 (electric piano fragment enters, filtered) -> Drop 1 (groove fills out, synth hook layered under piano) -> Breakdown (piano fragment fully exposed, bass drops out) -> Build 2 (bass and hats return, tension riser) -> Drop 2 (full groove, piano and hook interlocking) -> Outro (bass fades, piano fragment lingers alone)
soundDesignPrompt: A dubby, slightly delayed bassline with a soft-edged tone and long decay tail that bleeds into the next beat; sparse, airy shuffled hats with generous space between hits; a muted electric-piano fragment (Rhodes-style, chorused) playing a wandering minor-key phrase that never fully resolves; a subtle plucked synth hook sits low in the mix, mostly felt rather than heard, until the second drop.
mixMasterNotes: Let the electric piano's chorus and delay tails breathe with extra reverb headroom — this track should feel spacious even at club volume; keep the bass dub-delay tucked just under the piano so it doesn't smear the harmony.
djTransitionNotes: Pairs well with tracks 4 and 6 in a deepening set; the fully exposed piano breakdown is a strong moment to bring the lights down or layer an a cappella-free instrumental from another deck; outro's lingering piano gives a soft landing for a slow transition.
stylePrompt: Dubby tech-house, 123 BPM, delayed soft bassline, chorused electric-piano fragment, airy shuffled hats, subtle plucked synth hook, smoky atmospheric club groove, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: Soft plumes of plum-tinted smoke drift behind a pane of glass etched with faint gold circuitry, the glass fogging and clearing in time with the electric piano phrase, black velvet darkness framing the edges.
filmPrompt: A slow lateral pan across a smoke-filled glass partition lit from behind in warm gold, the smoke swirling lazily as the piano fragment plays, the camera never quite reaching the other side.
imagePrompt: A pane of frosted glass etched with delicate gold circuit-jewellery patterns, warm light glowing behind it through curling plum-tinted smoke, deep black velvet surroundings, moody atmospheric cinematography, square composition, no people, no text

title: Midnight Mainline
description: A driving, club-forward groove with a relentless rolling bassline and a stabbing jazz chord pattern that pushes the energy steadily upward.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (rolling bass, tight hats) -> Build 1 (jazz stab pattern locks in) -> Drop 1 (full groove, synth hook enters above stabs) -> Breakdown (stabs isolated, percussion stripped to claps) -> Build 2 (bass returns with sub layer, riser) -> Drop 2 (peak energy, hook and stabs interlocked) -> Outro (bass and hats only, gradual fade)
soundDesignPrompt: A rolling 16th-note bassline with a driving, slightly overdriven tone for club punch; tight, fast-shuffled hats layered with a crisp rim click for definition; a repeating jazz chord stab voiced with extended 9th and 11th intervals, panned subtly for width; a bright but short plucked synth hook that answers the stabs in call-and-response, acting as the track's lead voice.
mixMasterNotes: This is a true club-translation track — keep the kick and bass tight and controlled in the low-mids so it doesn't muddy on a big rig, and leave headroom for the stabs to punch through without clipping.
djTransitionNotes: Strong peak-time-adjacent track; compatible with 124-126 BPM material; the clap-only breakdown is an ideal point to bring in a parallel bassline from the next track for a layered transition.
stylePrompt: Driving tech-house, 125 BPM, rolling overdriven bassline, extended jazz 9th/11th chord stabs, tight shuffled hats, bright plucked synth hook, club-forward momentum, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: Sharp gold circuit-lines accelerate across a black velvet plane, branching faster with each stab, deep plum light strobing softly at the corners as the pattern builds toward a dense glowing lattice.
filmPrompt: A fast lateral tracking shot along a strip of gold-lit club railing, the camera speed increasing with the bassline, plum haze streaking past as the energy builds toward the drop.
imagePrompt: A dense lattice of glowing gold circuit-jewellery lines accelerating across black velvet, deep plum strobe-light accents at the edges, dynamic motion-blur energy, cinematic high-contrast lighting, square composition, no people, no text

title: Gold Dust Descent
description: The final climb before peak groove — a shimmering arpeggio cascades downward over a heavy, confident bassline, like gold dust falling through black light.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (descending arpeggio alone, no percussion) -> Build 1 (kick and hats enter under arpeggio) -> Drop 1 (full bassline, jazz stab accents) -> Breakdown (arpeggio isolated, pitched down and filtered) -> Build 2 (long riser, percussion stacks) -> Drop 2 (peak energy, arpeggio and bass fully combined) -> Outro (bass holds, arpeggio fades upward and out)
soundDesignPrompt: A heavy, confident bassline with a rounded sub layer and a slight growl on the upper harmonics; fast shuffled hats with a rolling open-hat pattern for forward motion; a bright, bell-like arpeggio synth cascading downward through a minor scale, run through a long stereo delay for shimmer; a single jazz stab used sparingly as a rhythmic anchor rather than a melodic element.
mixMasterNotes: Let the arpeggio's delay trails extend into the stereo field for shimmer and width, but keep the bassline mono and centered for club translation; this track should feel like it's gathering height — leave dynamic headroom for the peak tracks that follow.
djTransitionNotes: Ideal pre-peak riser track; compatible with 124-126 BPM peak material; the arpeggio-only intro is excellent for a long blend into the next track's drop, and the isolated breakdown arpeggio works well pitched against a new bassline.
stylePrompt: Tech-house, 125 BPM, cascading bell-like arpeggio with long delay shimmer, heavy growling bassline, rolling shuffled hats, sparse jazz stab accent, anticipatory peak-building energy, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: Cascading particles of gold light fall slowly through a black velvet void, scattering into plum-lit ripples on impact, the pattern accelerating and brightening as the track climbs toward its peak.
filmPrompt: A slow-motion shot of golden dust falling through a shaft of light in a dark club interior, the camera tilting upward to follow the particles as the arpeggio cascades, plum shadow deepening around the edges.
imagePrompt: Golden dust particles suspended mid-fall through a beam of warm light in a black velvet void, deep plum shadow at the edges, faint circuit-pattern jewellery glinting among the falling light, painterly slow-motion atmosphere, square composition, no people, no text

title: Circuit Heat
description: Peak groove begins — a hard-edged, distorted bassline collides with a syncopated synth hook for the most physically driving track on the album.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (distorted bass stabs, tight hats) -> Build 1 (synth hook enters syncopated against bass) -> Drop 1 (full peak groove, jazz stab layered high) -> Breakdown (hook isolated, percussion drops to claps and rim) -> Build 2 (bass and hats stack, tension riser) -> Drop 2 (hardest peak moment, all elements combined) -> Outro (bass and hook trade off, gradual strip-back)
soundDesignPrompt: A hard-edged, distorted bassline with aggressive saturation and a short, percussive envelope for maximum club impact; fast, tight shuffled hats with layered rim and clap accents for density; a syncopated synth hook using a sharp pluck timbre that deliberately falls off the beat from the bassline to create rhythmic tension; a high jazz chord stab used as a bright textural layer rather than a rhythmic one.
mixMasterNotes: This is the hardest-hitting track on the album — push bass saturation for grit but watch sub buildup so the low end stays controlled on big systems; leave just enough headroom on the hook for it to cut cleanly through a packed, loud room.
djTransitionNotes: Core peak-time track, 125-126 BPM compatible; the claps-only breakdown is a strong drop-out point for crowd response; works well mixed back-to-back with tracks 9 and 10 for a sustained peak block.
stylePrompt: Peak-time dark tech-house, 126 BPM, hard distorted bassline, syncopated sharp synth hook, dense shuffled hats with claps, bright jazz stab texture, aggressive physical club energy, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: Jagged gold circuit-bolts flash erratically across a black velvet field in sync with the syncopated hook, deep plum light flaring hard on every bass hit, the whole frame vibrating with barely-contained tension.
filmPrompt: Rapid handheld-style cuts between flashes of gold strobe light and black velvet darkness, the camera shaking subtly on each distorted bass hit, plum light bursts punctuating the syncopated rhythm.
imagePrompt: Jagged bolts of gold circuit-jewellery light flashing across deep black velvet, hard plum light flares at the points of contact, high-energy strobe-lit composition, dramatic dark club atmosphere, square composition, no people, no text

title: Obsidian Floor
description: The album's darkest, most hypnotic peak moment — a minimal, rolling groove stripped to bass, hats, and one relentless stab loop.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (rolling bass and hats only, stab loop teased) -> Build 1 (stab loop locks fully in) -> Drop 1 (full minimal peak groove) -> Breakdown (everything drops to bass alone, near-silence) -> Build 2 (hats and stab return, slow rebuild) -> Drop 2 (hardest, most minimal peak moment) -> Outro (stab loop alone, bass fades under it)
soundDesignPrompt: A relentless, rolling bassline with minimal variation, designed for hypnotic repetition rather than melody; dry, tight shuffled hats with almost no reverb for a claustrophobic, close feel; a single dark jazz chord stab looped with subtle rhythmic variation every 8 bars, run through light analog-style drive for grit; no synth hook lead — the stab itself functions as the track's only melodic-harmonic voice, deliberately minimal to maximize the loop's hypnotic pull.
mixMasterNotes: Mix this dry and tight — minimal reverb, minimal stereo width — to create a claustrophobic peak-time intensity that contrasts with the more open earlier tracks; the near-silent breakdown should be mixed loud enough in just the bass that it still feels powerful at low perceived loudness.
djTransitionNotes: The most minimal, loopable track on the album — ideal for extended peak-time mixing and long blends; 125-126 BPM compatible; the near-silent bass-only breakdown is a strong moment for a dramatic crowd peak or lighting cue.
stylePrompt: Minimal dark tech-house, 126 BPM, relentless rolling bassline, single looping jazz stab with subtle 8-bar variation, dry tight shuffled hats, hypnotic claustrophobic peak-time groove, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: A single deep-black obsidian disc rotates slowly at the center of the frame, its surface catching thin gold circuit-veins that pulse with the stab loop, plum light barely visible at the very edges of total darkness.
filmPrompt: An extreme slow zoom into a black obsidian surface reflecting a single point of gold light, the camera barely moving as the stab loop repeats, the frame almost entirely dark except for that one glint.
imagePrompt: A polished black obsidian surface reflecting a single thin vein of gold circuit-light, near-total darkness surrounding it with the faintest hint of plum at the edges, minimalist high-contrast macro photography, square composition, no people, no text

title: Gilt Frenzy
description: The album's energetic high point — layered synth hooks stack into a dense, glittering peak groove with the busiest percussion arrangement on the record.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (busy shuffled percussion, bass pulse) -> Build 1 (first synth hook layer enters) -> Drop 1 (full groove, two hook layers interlocking) -> Breakdown (hooks strip to one, piano fragment flickers underneath) -> Build 2 (all percussion and hooks stack back in) -> Drop 2 (climactic peak, three-layer hook stack) -> Outro (hooks peel away one by one over bass and hats)
soundDesignPrompt: A punchy, driving bassline with a tight sidechain-style pump feel for rhythmic propulsion; the busiest percussion arrangement on the album — shuffled hats, congas, and rim clicks interlocking in a dense polyrhythmic pattern; two interlocking plucked synth hooks in different registers (one bright and high, one warm and mid) that stack at the climax into a glittering three-part texture with a brief smoky piano fragment flickering beneath; a jazz stab used as rhythmic glue between hook phrases.
mixMasterNotes: With this many layered elements, prioritize frequency separation — keep each hook in its own EQ lane so the climax reads as glittering and dense rather than cluttered; this is the loudest, most maximal moment on the album and should use the most available headroom.
djTransitionNotes: Best mixed at the absolute peak of a set; 125-126 BPM compatible; the single-hook breakdown is a good point to bring a second track's percussion in early for an extended dense transition.
stylePrompt: Maximal peak-time tech-house, 126 BPM, two interlocking plucked synth hooks, polyrhythmic shuffled percussion with congas, punchy pumping bassline, smoky piano flicker, jazz stab glue, glittering club climax, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: A glittering web of gold circuit-threads weaves and multiplies across black velvet, each new hook layer adding another interlocking strand, deep plum bursts flashing through the gaps as the pattern reaches maximum density.
filmPrompt: A sweeping crane shot rising over a dense crowd of gold light fixtures pulsing in layered rhythm, the camera accelerating upward as the hooks stack, plum light bursts strobing through the gold lattice.
imagePrompt: A dense, glittering web of interlocking gold circuit-jewellery threads against black velvet, bursts of deep plum light flashing through the gaps, maximalist ornate composition, rich cinematic glow, square composition, no people, no text

title: Afterglow Lounge
description: The wind-down begins — peak elements melt into a warm, slow-shuffling groove built around a single sustained jazz chord and a tape-warmed bassline.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (sustained jazz chord pad, soft bass pulse) -> Build 1 (shuffled hats enter gently) -> Drop 1 (groove settles, synth hook drifts in softly) -> Breakdown (piano fragment solo, percussion drops away) -> Build 2 (hats and bass return, gentle) -> Drop 2 (final settled groove, warm and unhurried) -> Outro (chord pad sustains, everything else fades)
soundDesignPrompt: A tape-warmed bassline with soft saturation and rounded transients for an unhurried, glowing feel; gentle, loosely shuffled hats with more space and less density than the peak tracks; a sustained jazz chord pad (minor 7th, slow attack) that anchors the whole track harmonically; a soft, slow synth hook that drifts rather than punches, mirroring the released tension of the room; a smoky piano fragment used melodically and generously in the breakdown as the emotional center of the track.
mixMasterNotes: Warm everything up here — gentle tape-style saturation across the bus, slightly more reverb than the peak tracks, and a lower overall loudness target to signal the energy coming down without losing club weight.
djTransitionNotes: First wind-down track; compatible with 122-125 BPM material; the generous piano breakdown is an ideal point to start dropping percussion from other decks; long sustained outro chord allows for a very slow, gradual transition.
stylePrompt: Warm tech-house wind-down, 124 BPM, tape-saturated bassline, sustained minor-7th jazz chord pad, gentle shuffled hats, drifting synth hook, generous smoky piano fragment, relaxed afterglow groove, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: A warm gold glow spreads slowly outward like spilled light across black velvet, deep plum tones softening at the edges, the circuit-pattern lines relaxing into loose, organic curves instead of sharp angles.
filmPrompt: A slow, steady pull-back from a single warmly lit booth as the room empties of motion, gold light pooling softer and wider, plum shadow gently overtaking the edges of frame in an unhurried fade.
imagePrompt: Warm gold light spilling softly across rumpled black velvet fabric, deep plum shadow gently overtaking the edges, the circuit-jewellery pattern rendered in soft relaxed curves rather than sharp lines, tranquil painterly afterglow, square composition, no people, no text

title: Last Call Velvet
description: The closing track — the album's most minimal groove, dissolving slowly into a single piano fragment and silence, the lights coming up gently.
arrangementPrompt: Intro (soft bass pulse, distant shuffled hats) -> Build 1 (smoky piano fragment enters, sparse) -> Drop 1 (gentle groove, minimal synth hook, low energy) -> Breakdown (piano fragment alone, bass barely present) -> Build 2 (the faintest return of hats and bass) -> Drop 2 (final, softest groove statement) -> Outro (piano fragment fades alone into silence)
soundDesignPrompt: A barely-there bassline, soft and rounded, present more as warmth than rhythm; distant, sparse shuffled hats mixed low and far back; a smoky piano fragment that becomes the true lead voice of the track, playing a slow, unresolved phrase that repeats with small variations; a faint, breathy synth hook appears only once, in the final drop, like a last memory of the album's hook motifs before everything fades.
mixMasterNotes: Mix for intimacy — wide dynamic range, generous quiet headroom, minimal compression so the piano fragment can breathe naturally; this is the lounge end of the album's spectrum and should sound beautiful at low volume as the room empties.
djTransitionNotes: Closing track only; works at 121-124 BPM for a gentle final blend out of the set; long unaccompanied piano outro is designed to be allowed to play out in full, ideally unmixed, as the set's final statement.
stylePrompt: Minimal tech-house closer, 122 BPM, sparse rounded bassline, distant shuffled hats, lead smoky piano fragment, single faint breathy synth hook, tender unresolved fade, intimate velvet wind-down, instrumental only, no vocals, no lyrics, no words
visualizerPrompt: A single soft gold light slowly dims at the center of an entirely black velvet frame, the faint circuit-jewellery pattern fading strand by strand into darkness, the last plum glow disappearing like a held breath released.
filmPrompt: A static shot of a single dimming gold light in an emptying club, the camera holding perfectly still as shadows lengthen and the plum glow fades to black, the final frame held in near-total darkness.
